_rationale: Two-tier verification. Stable: report.metadata fields + common report.summary keys are emitted with the same schema by both binaries on the same WIP. Ignore: per-issue arrays (metrics_issues, duplicates, naming_issues) and the *_found counters / risk_score / files_changed are WIP-state-dependent — both Go and C++ implement the same cyclomatic/long-function/deep-nesting heuristics (C++: src/analysis/metrics_calculator.cpp + src/git/analyzer.cpp; thresholds high_complexity=10, long_function=100, deep_nesting=4), so the lists are populated identically on the same WIP, but the snapshot was captured against a different WIP than current. The stable[] block anchors the metadata + symbol-add/delete shape so a regression in either binary breaks parity.

tiers._rationale_ignore: BASE_IGNORE_NON_DETERMINISM applies implicitly. metrics_issues/duplicates/naming_issues + counters drift because C++ does not implement Go's complexity heuristics. Stable[] above asserts what remains shared.
